title: Battle of Sluis Van
content:

**[[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']] *4 [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyers]] **''[[Death's Head]]'' **[[Inexorable (Imperial I-class)|''Inexorable'']] **''[[Nemesis]]'' **[[Stormhawk (Imperial I-class)|''Stormhawk'']] *12 [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class medium cruisers]] *22 [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|''Carrack''-class light cruisers]] *Many [[Zero-G assault stormtrooper|Spacetroopers]] *Many [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=Around 43 crippled capital ships |casual2=*All mole miners and crews *Some TIEs *Some Spacetroopers |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|Remember—we're here to gain ships, not lose them.''"
"''And captain…''"
"''Yes, Admiral?''"
"''Remind them that our final victory over the Rebellion begins here.|Grand Admiral Thrawn and Captain Pellaeon|}} The '''Battle of Sluis Van''', also known as the '''Battle at Sluis Van Shipyards''', was the first major military conflict of the [[Thrawn campaign]], orchestrated by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] to steal a flotilla of [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] ships. ==Background== The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]] began with feints at [[Bpfassh]] and [[Draukyze]] which would ultimately lead to large numbers of ships (which would otherwise have been in these systems) being present in the [[Sluis system]] later. The hit-and-fade tactics also forced the New Republic to use its starfighter squadrons to become escort craft along various shipping routes to defend against possible Imperial attacks, and left the New Republic strapped for freighters. This also caused the New Republic to strip down and give only skeleton crews to some larger war vessels, even [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruisers]], to transport goods and supplies. Thrawn also [[Raid on Nomad City|attacked]] [[Nkllon/Legends|Nkllon]], stealing fifty-one [[Plasma-Jet Mole Miner|mole miners]] from [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian's]] [[Nomad City]], jamming the system so that the administrator could not transmit his override codes. Finally, Thrawn's acquisition of [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking]] technology gave him the extra help he would need to smuggle his ships into the system. ==Battle== Hours before the battle began, [[Cloak Leader]] was dispatched in an [[A-class bulk freighter|''A''-class bulk freighter]], the ''[[Nartissteu]]'', which had a [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]] installed ''inside'' its cargo hold. When activated, the ship remained fully visible to both scanners and the naked eye, but the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and mole miners within appeared as empty space to any scans performed on the freighter. [[File:CloakSquadron-HTTE6.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Cloak Squadron bursts from the cargo hold of ''Nartissteu'']] Although to the eyes and battle senses of the seasoned New Republic officers [[Afyon]] and [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] the ship still seemed strange, the ''Nartissteu'' and its hidden fighter complement were able to fool [[Sluis Van Central space station|Sluis Van Central]] and [[Sluis Control]] by reporting damage sustained in a pirate attack and being forced to drop their cargo and run. Antilles, deciding that the freighter handled more like a full, functional ship than a damaged, empty one, ordered [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] to launch from Afyon's ''[[Larkhess]]''. The ''Nartissteu'' exploded as the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] reached it, with the command section piloting away and the destroyed hull leaving space open for forty TIEs and fifty mole miners to engage the under-defended fleet of undermanned New Republic ships. At this point Thrawn's task force emerged from hyperspace to attack the spaceyard's perimeter. His thirty-nine capital warships and thirty TIE squadrons had the conservative goal of engaging and pinning down the formidable New Republic defenses, thus reducing the pressure on the Imperial infiltrators. The timely arrival of the Rogues and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] in the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] hardly slowed the Imperial forces as the mole miners drilled through the New Republic ships' hulls to place boarding parties. Thrawn had packed four or five [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and [[Zero-G assault stormtrooper|spacetroopers]] into each of the two-man mining craft. As each ship only had a skeleton crew while under repair, these boarders were efficient enough to have even taken control of at least one Star Cruiser in only minutes, firing at Commander Antilles as he attempted to use what he thought was an allied ship as cover. The Imperials were even tapping into Republic comms, listening to Solo and Antilles' tactics, so as to defend the mole miners against combined attacks. ==Thrawn's oversight== [[File:Sluis_van_2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Plasma-Jet Mole Miner|Mole miners]] drilling through the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] ships]] Before the raiders could abscond with the New Republic fleet, Han Solo realized that the Imperials were not jamming their comms this time, and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] used his command codes to activate the miners, making them drill entirely ''through'' the fleeing starships. However, at least one winged [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari cruiser]], three [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transports]], four [[CR90 corvette/Legends|Corellian Corvettes]] and one [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B frigate]] were crippled by the miners' engines and a self-destructive spacetrooper before they were stopped. His plan foiled, Thrawn ordered [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']] to retreat, telling Captain [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Pellaeon]] that the New Republic might have won, but had lost a great many ships in the process. ==Appearances== *''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' {{Ab|Heir to the Empire (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *[[Heir to the Empire 6|''Heir to the Empire'' 6]] *''[[Dark Force Rising]]'' {{Ab|Dark Force Rising (unabridged audiobook)}} *''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Jedi Search]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]]'' {{1stID|Battle of Sluis Van & Battle at Sluis Van Shipyards}} *''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]'' *''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Movie Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]'' *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *{{FactFile|3|ACK 1-4|Admiral Ackbar}} *{{FactFile|5|THR 1-4|Grand Admiral Thrawn}} *{{InsiderCite|59|Who's Who in Rogue Squadron}} *{{FactFile|15|NEB 1-2|Nebulon-B Frigate}} *{{FactFile|97|THR 1-4|The Thrawn Campaign}} *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'' *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GCW_Thrawn}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht von Sluis Van#legends |es=Batalla de Sluis Van |it=Battaglia di Sluis Van |hu=Sluis Van-i csata }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sluis Van, Battle of}} [[Category:Battles of Sluis Van]] [[Category:Battles of the Thrawn campaign]]
